title: Wildberries: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on retail warehouses
excerpt: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.
content_html:
1 day ago

Sofia Volyanova,

Yaroslava Kiryukhina,

Amalia Zatariand

Tatiana Kovtun,BBC News Russian

Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer, Wildberries, in a matter of days, dealing a blow to a large number of small and medium-sized businesses.

After months of drone attacks on oil depots and refineries, Kyiv says it is now targeting logistics hubs used by Russians to buy military equipment, and disrupting supplies to the Russian army.

But it has also frustrated hundreds of Russian sellers who depend on online marketplaces for their livelihoods, making the war feel closer to home after almost four and a half years.

"I am a mother of a disabled child, I've built the business myself," one woman complained on an expletive-laden social media post that went viral.

"I've lost 1m roubles (£9,500; $12,700)... We're already getting screwed over from every direction as it is."

In Ukraine's first attack on Wildberries at the weekend, two warehouses were hit, at Elektrostal east of Moscow and in Kotovsk in the Tambov region, some 400km (250 miles) from the Ukrainian border.

Seven workers on a nightshift were killed in Kotovsk and one was fatally wounded at Elektrostal. Dozens more were injured and goods worth an estimated at £750m ($1bn) were destroyed.

Kyiv said it had struck "major logistics facilities" used to "supply sanctioned components for drone production and navigation equipment".

More logistical sites were targeted early on Monday, including another Wildberries warehouse in Koledino south of Moscow. Further attacks overnight into Wednesday hit Wildberries logistic hubs at Krasnodar and Nevinnomyssk in the Stavropol region.

The company's owner Tatiana Kim said further casualties had been reported.

"We are, quite justly, bringing the war back home - to Russia," said Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky.

Russia has targeted Ukrainian cities with drones and missiles since the start of the full-scale invasion in February 2022 and recent Russian attacks have hit Ukrainian warehouses and cargo terminals.

Sorting terminals for Ukraine's largest private postal operator, Nova Poshta, have come under attack, so too have warehouses owned by retail chain ATB and confectionery group Roshen.

With the situation on the front line now close to a stalemate, Ukraine's long-distance drone campaign has hit supply routes and energy facilities, in effect cutting off annexed Crimea and leading to fuel shortages across Russia.

Businesses had already been disrupted before the warehouse attacks, but now some have lost much of their stock overnight.

Wildberries and its rival Ozon are used by 85-90% of Russia's economically active population, it is estimated. In many remote places lacking accessible stores, Russians depend on them for much of their shopping, from clothes to household appliances.

The Kremlin has denied that online platforms are used for army supplies, but a variety of military-related goods such as body armour are on sale, alongside items with both civilian and drone-production applications.

Pro-war bloggers say the platforms are routinely relied on by volunteers to buy kit that is then sent to the front.

There is, however, no independent confirmation that the warehouses targeted in the attacks stored these supplies.

Unlike Western retail giant Amazon, which sells goods directly alongside its marketplace business, Wildberries and Ozon act almost entirely as marketplaces, connecting independent sellers with buyers across Russia, storing and delivering their goods and handling payments.

This means many of the losses incurred in the attacks have been borne by businesses.

For one woman, who sells stationery items, Wildberries is the only distribution channel. The day after she delivered her goods to the Elektrostal warehouse near Moscow last Friday, she learned that it had all gone up in flames.

She still has other inventory left, but this time of the year is important for her business, as parents and students stock up for the new autumn term.

Another Wildberries trader who sells teenage clothing, told the BBC she had lost about 1.4m roubles (£13,300) worth of stock. She is halting new supplies for a few weeks.

"The outlook for working with Wildberries is very murky," she said. "Right now I have enough of a safety cushion to survive the current losses, but if there are more strikes, I probably won't manage."

Neither woman expects compensation. Earlier this month, less than two weeks before the attacks, a clause was added to their contracts, including drone attacks in the list of "force majeure events" for which Wildberries is not liable.

Company owner Tatiana Kim has promised "not to abandon" the sellers and some compensation has been paid to a number of small businesses. She has also offered temporary discounts and waived shipment fees, but this has done little to calm fears of further attacks.

Supporting small and medium-sized businesses has been among Vladimir Putin's declared priorities even in wartime, and framed as a backbone of resilience in the face of Western sanctions.

But the Russian state has been unable to shield the sector from the war's impact, and there have been several shocks to businesses since the start of the year.

VAT went up from 20% to 22% in January, with extra funding earmarked for defence, and tax breaks for some businesses were scrapped.

Some 209,000 small and medium-sized companies closed in the first quarter of 2026, according to business intelligence platform Kontur.Fokus. That is 9% more than in the first three months of 2025.

Widespread internet shutdowns and a crackdown on popular messaging apps led to further troubles, and Moscow businesses lost tens of millions of dollars in one week in March, according to some estimates.

And then came the fuel crisis, triggered by Ukrainian strikes on oil depots, refineries and supply routes.

"There are already a lot of nails, and they keep hammering them in. Is [the attack on Wildberries warehouses] another nail in the coffin? Well, yes, of course," Prof Ruben Eniklopov of Pompeu Fabra University in Barcelona told the BBC.

Small businesses in Russia have proved resilient in the past, but inflation is high, the budget deficit is widening, and oil and gas revenues are 23% lower than in the first six months of last year.

Russia has vast financial reserves, accumulated in peacetime, but its war in Ukraine is increasingly swallowing resources, starving the civilian economy of growth.

Additional reporting by Olga Shamina.

sentences.en: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer, Wildberries, in a matter of days, dealing a blow to a large number of small and medium-sized businesses.
sentences.zh: 乌克兰无人机在几天内袭击了俄罗斯最大在线零售商Wildberry旗下的多个零售仓库，对大量中小企业造成打击。

blocks.html_en: After months of drone attacks on oil depots and refineries, Kyiv says it is now targeting logistics hubs used by Russians to buy military equipment, and disrupting supplies to the Russian army.

blocks.html_en: But it has also frustrated hundreds of Russian sellers who depend on online marketplaces for their livelihoods, making the war feel closer to home after almost four and a half years.

sentences.en: But it has also frustrated hundreds of Russian sellers who depend on online marketplaces for their livelihoods, making the war feel closer to home after almost four and a half years.
sentences.zh: 但这也让数百名依赖在线市场谋生的俄罗斯卖家感到沮丧，让近四年半后的战争感觉离家更近了。

blocks.html_en: "I am a mother of a disabled child, I've built the business myself," one woman complained on an expletive-laden social media post that went viral.

blocks.html_en: "I've lost 1m roubles (£9,500; $12,700)... We're already getting screwed over from every direction as it is."

We're already getting screwed over from every direction as it is."
sentences.zh: 我们已经从各个方向被搞砸了。”

blocks.html_en: In Ukraine's first attack on Wildberries at the weekend, two warehouses were hit, at Elektrostal east of Moscow and in Kotovsk in the Tambov region, some 400km (250 miles) from the Ukrainian border.

sentences.en: In Ukraine's first attack on Wildberries at the weekend, two warehouses were hit, at Elektrostal east of Moscow and in Kotovsk in the Tambov region, some 400km (250 miles) from the Ukrainian border.
sentences.zh: 周末，乌克兰首次对 Wildberry 进行袭击，两个仓库遭到袭击，分别位于莫斯科以东的 Elektrostal 和坦波夫地区的 Kotovsk，距离乌克兰边境约 400 公里（250 英里）。

blocks.html_en: Seven workers on a nightshift were killed in Kotovsk and one was fatally wounded at Elektrostal. Dozens more were injured and goods worth an estimated at £750m ($1bn) were destroyed.

sentences.en: Seven workers on a nightshift were killed in Kotovsk and one was fatally wounded at Elektrostal.
sentences.zh: 七名夜班工人在科托夫斯克被杀，一名在埃莱克特罗斯塔受重伤。

sentences.en: Dozens more were injured and goods worth an estimated at £750m ($1bn) were destroyed.
sentences.zh: 另有数十人受伤，价值估计 7.5 亿英镑（10 亿美元）的货物被毁。

blocks.html_en: Kyiv said it had struck "major logistics facilities" used to "supply sanctioned components for drone production and navigation equipment".

sentences.en: Kyiv said it had struck "major logistics facilities" used to "supply sanctioned components for drone production and navigation equipment".
sentences.zh: 基辅表示，它袭击了用于“为无人机生产和导航设备供应受制裁部件”的“主要物流设施”。

blocks.html_en: More logistical sites were targeted early on Monday, including another Wildberries warehouse in Koledino south of Moscow. Further attacks overnight into Wednesday hit Wildberries logistic hubs at Krasnodar and Nevinnomyssk in the Stavropol region.

sentences.en: More logistical sites were targeted early on Monday, including another Wildberries warehouse in Koledino south of Moscow.
sentences.zh: 周一早些时候，更多的物流地点成为攻击目标，其中包括位于莫斯科南部科莱迪诺的另一个 Wildberry 仓库。

sentences.en: Further attacks overnight into Wednesday hit Wildberries logistic hubs at Krasnodar and Nevinnomyssk in the Stavropol region.
sentences.zh: 周三夜间，斯塔夫罗波尔地区克拉斯诺达尔和涅文诺梅斯克的 Wildberry 物流中心遭受了进一步袭击。

blocks.html_en: The company's owner Tatiana Kim said further casualties had been reported.

sentences.en: The company's owner Tatiana Kim said further casualties had been reported.
sentences.zh: 该公司老板塔蒂安娜·金 (Tatiana Kim) 表示，据报道还有更多人员伤亡。

blocks.html_en: "We are, quite justly, bringing the war back home - to Russia," said Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky.

sentences.en: "We are, quite justly, bringing the war back home - to Russia," said Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky.
sentences.zh: 乌克兰总统泽连斯基表示：“我们非常公正地将战争带回国内——俄罗斯。”

blocks.html_en: Russia has targeted Ukrainian cities with drones and missiles since the start of the full-scale invasion in February 2022 and recent Russian attacks have hit Ukrainian warehouses and cargo terminals.

sentences.en: Russia has targeted Ukrainian cities with drones and missiles since the start of the full-scale invasion in February 2022 and recent Russian attacks have hit Ukrainian warehouses and cargo terminals.
sentences.zh: 自 2022 年 2 月开始全面入侵以来，俄罗斯一直用无人机和导弹瞄准乌克兰城市，最近俄罗斯的袭击袭击了乌克兰的仓库和货运站。

blocks.html_en: Sorting terminals for Ukraine's largest private postal operator, Nova Poshta, have come under attack, so too have warehouses owned by retail chain ATB and confectionery group Roshen.

sentences.en: Sorting terminals for Ukraine's largest private postal operator, Nova Poshta, have come under attack, so too have warehouses owned by retail chain ATB and confectionery group Roshen.
sentences.zh: 乌克兰最大的私营邮政运营商 Nova Poshta 的分拣终端受到攻击，零售连锁店 ATB 和糖果集团 Roshen 拥有的仓库也受到攻击。

blocks.html_en: With the situation on the front line now close to a stalemate, Ukraine's long-distance drone campaign has hit supply routes and energy facilities, in effect cutting off annexed Crimea and leading to fuel shortages across Russia.

sentences.en: With the situation on the front line now close to a stalemate, Ukraine's long-distance drone campaign has hit supply routes and energy facilities, in effect cutting off annexed Crimea and leading to fuel shortages across Russia.
sentences.zh: 由于前线局势现已接近僵局，乌克兰的远程无人机袭击袭击了补给路线和能源设施，实际上切断了所吞并的克里米亚的联系，并导致俄罗斯各地的燃料短缺。

blocks.html_en: Businesses had already been disrupted before the warehouse attacks, but now some have lost much of their stock overnight.

sentences.en: Businesses had already been disrupted before the warehouse attacks, but now some have lost much of their stock overnight.
sentences.zh: 在仓库袭击之前，企业就已经受到干扰，但现在有些企业一夜之间失去了大部分库存。

blocks.html_en: Wildberries and its rival Ozon are used by 85-90% of Russia's economically active population, it is estimated. In many remote places lacking accessible stores, Russians depend on them for much of their shopping, from clothes to household appliances.

sentences.zh: 据估计，俄罗斯 85-90% 的经济活跃人口都使用 Wildberry 及其竞争对手 Ozon。

sentences.en: In many remote places lacking accessible stores, Russians depend on them for much of their shopping, from clothes to household appliances.
sentences.zh: 在许多缺乏便利商店的偏远地区，俄罗斯人的大部分购物都依赖它们，从衣服到家用电器。

sentences.zh: “与 Wildberry 合作的前景非常暗淡，”她说。

sentences.en: "Right now I have enough of a safety cushion to survive the current losses, but if there are more strikes, I probably won't manage."
sentences.zh: “现在我有足够的安全缓冲来承受当前的损失，但如果有更多的罢工，我可能无法应对。”

blocks.html_en: Neither woman expects compensation. Earlier this month, less than two weeks before the attacks, a clause was added to their contracts, including drone attacks in the list of "force majeure events" for which Wildberries is not liable.

sentences.en: Neither woman expects compensation.
sentences.zh: 两个女人都不期望得到补偿。

sentences.en: Earlier this month, less than two weeks before the attacks, a clause was added to their contracts, including drone attacks in the list of "force majeure events" for which Wildberries is not liable.
sentences.zh: 本月早些时候，距离袭击事件发生不到两周，他们的合同中添加了一项条款，其中将无人机袭击列入“不可抗力事件”清单，而 Wildberry 对此不承担责任。

blocks.html_en: Company owner Tatiana Kim has promised "not to abandon" the sellers and some compensation has been paid to a number of small businesses. She has also offered temporary discounts and waived shipment fees, but this has done little to calm fears of further attacks.

sentences.en: Company owner Tatiana Kim has promised "not to abandon" the sellers and some compensation has been paid to a number of small businesses.
sentences.zh: 公司老板塔蒂亚娜·金 (Tatiana Kim) 承诺“不会放弃”卖家，并向一些小企业支付了一些补偿。

sentences.en: She has also offered temporary discounts and waived shipment fees, but this has done little to calm fears of further attacks.
sentences.zh: 她还提供了临时折扣并免除了运费，但这并没有缓解人们对进一步袭击的担忧。

blocks.html_en: Supporting small and medium-sized businesses has been among Vladimir Putin's declared priorities even in wartime, and framed as a backbone of resilience in the face of Western sanctions.

sentences.en: Supporting small and medium-sized businesses has been among Vladimir Putin's declared priorities even in wartime, and framed as a backbone of resilience in the face of Western sanctions.
sentences.zh: 即使在战时，支持中小企业也一直是弗拉基米尔·普京宣布的优先事项之一，并被视为面对西方制裁时的韧性支柱。

blocks.html_en: But the Russian state has been unable to shield the sector from the war's impact, and there have been several shocks to businesses since the start of the year.

sentences.en: But the Russian state has been unable to shield the sector from the war's impact, and there have been several shocks to businesses since the start of the year.
sentences.zh: 但俄罗斯政府一直无法保护该行业免受战争的影响，自今年年初以来，企业遭受了数次冲击。

blocks.html_en: VAT went up from 20% to 22% in January, with extra funding earmarked for defence, and tax breaks for some businesses were scrapped.

sentences.en: VAT went up from 20% to 22% in January, with extra funding earmarked for defence, and tax breaks for some businesses were scrapped.
sentences.zh: 1 月份增值税从 20% 上涨至 22%，额外资金专门用于国防，并且取消了一些企业的税收优惠。

blocks.html_en: Some 209,000 small and medium-sized companies closed in the first quarter of 2026, according to business intelligence platform Kontur.Fokus. That is 9% more than in the first three months of 2025.

sentences.en: Some 209,000 small and medium-sized companies closed in the first quarter of 2026, according to business intelligence platform Kontur.Fokus.
sentences.zh: 根据商业智能平台 Kontur.Fokus 的数据，2026 年第一季度约有 209,000 家中小企业倒闭。

sentences.en: That is 9% more than in the first three months of 2025.
sentences.zh: 这比 2025 年前三个月增加了 9%。

blocks.html_en: Widespread internet shutdowns and a crackdown on popular messaging apps led to further troubles, and Moscow businesses lost tens of millions of dollars in one week in March, according to some estimates.

sentences.en: Widespread internet shutdowns and a crackdown on popular messaging apps led to further troubles, and Moscow businesses lost tens of millions of dollars in one week in March, according to some estimates.
sentences.zh: 据一些估计，大范围的互联网关闭和对流行通讯应用程序的打击导致了进一步的麻烦，莫斯科企业在 3 月份的一周内损失了数千万美元。

blocks.html_en: And then came the fuel crisis, triggered by Ukrainian strikes on oil depots, refineries and supply routes.

sentences.en: And then came the fuel crisis, triggered by Ukrainian strikes on oil depots, refineries and supply routes.
sentences.zh: 随后乌克兰对油库、炼油厂和供应路线的袭击引发了燃料危机。

blocks.html_en: "There are already a lot of nails, and they keep hammering them in. Is [the attack on Wildberries warehouses] another nail in the coffin? Well, yes, of course," Prof Ruben Eniklopov of Pompeu Fabra University in Barcelona told the BBC.

sentences.en: "There are already a lot of nails, and they keep hammering them in.
sentences.zh: “已经有很多钉子了，他们还在不断地钉钉子。

sentences.en: Is [the attack on Wildberries warehouses] another nail in the coffin?
sentences.zh: [Wildberry 仓库袭击事件] 是又一个致命一击吗？

sentences.en: Well, yes, of course," Prof Ruben Eniklopov of Pompeu Fabra University in Barcelona told the BBC.
sentences.zh: 嗯，是的，当然，”巴塞罗那庞培法布拉大学的鲁本·埃尼克洛波夫教授告诉英国广播公司。

blocks.html_en: Small businesses in Russia have proved resilient in the past, but inflation is high, the budget deficit is widening, and oil and gas revenues are 23% lower than in the first six months of last year.

sentences.en: Small businesses in Russia have proved resilient in the past, but inflation is high, the budget deficit is widening, and oil and gas revenues are 23% lower than in the first six months of last year.
sentences.zh: 过去，俄罗斯的小企业表现出了韧性，但通货膨胀率很高，预算赤字不断扩大，石油和天然气收入比去年前六个月下降了23%。

blocks.html_en: Russia has vast financial reserves, accumulated in peacetime, but its war in Ukraine is increasingly swallowing resources, starving the civilian economy of growth.

sentences.en: Russia has vast financial reserves, accumulated in peacetime, but its war in Ukraine is increasingly swallowing resources, starving the civilian economy of growth.
sentences.zh: 俄罗斯拥有和平时期积累的巨额财政储备，但其在乌克兰的战争正在日益吞噬资源，导致民用经济增长乏力。

blocks.html_en: Additional reporting by Olga Shamina.

sentences.en: Additional reporting by Olga Shamina.
sentences.zh: 奥尔加·沙米娜的补充报道。

content_html_zh:
1 天前

Sofia Volyanova、Yaroslava Kiryukhina、Amalia Zatarian 和 Tatiana Kovtun，BBC 俄语新闻

乌克兰无人机在几天内袭击了俄罗斯最大在线零售商Wildberry旗下的多个零售仓库，对大量中小企业造成打击。

基辅表示，在对油库和炼油厂进行了数月无人机袭击后，它现在的目标是俄罗斯人用来购买军事装备的物流中心，并中断了对俄罗斯军队的供应。

但这也让数百名依赖在线市场谋生的俄罗斯卖家感到沮丧，让近四年半后的战争感觉离家更近了。

“我是一名残疾孩子的母亲，我自己创办了这家公司，”一位女士在社交媒体上发布的一篇充满咒骂的帖子中抱怨道，该帖子在网上疯传。

“我损失了 100 万卢布（9,500 英镑；12,700 美元）...... 我们已经从各个方向被搞砸了。”

周末，乌克兰首次对 Wildberry 进行袭击，两个仓库遭到袭击，分别位于莫斯科以东的 Elektrostal 和坦波夫地区的 Kotovsk，距离乌克兰边境约 400 公里（250 英里）。

七名夜班工人在科托夫斯克被杀，一名在埃莱克特罗斯塔受重伤。 另有数十人受伤，价值估计 7.5 亿英镑（10 亿美元）的货物被毁。

基辅表示，它袭击了用于“为无人机生产和导航设备供应受制裁部件”的“主要物流设施”。

周一早些时候，更多的物流地点成为攻击目标，其中包括位于莫斯科南部科莱迪诺的另一个 Wildberry 仓库。 周三夜间，斯塔夫罗波尔地区克拉斯诺达尔和涅文诺梅斯克的 Wildberry 物流中心遭受了进一步袭击。

该公司老板塔蒂安娜·金 (Tatiana Kim) 表示，据报道还有更多人员伤亡。

乌克兰总统泽连斯基表示：“我们非常公正地将战争带回国内——俄罗斯。”

自 2022 年 2 月开始全面入侵以来，俄罗斯一直用无人机和导弹瞄准乌克兰城市，最近俄罗斯的袭击袭击了乌克兰的仓库和货运站。

乌克兰最大的私营邮政运营商 Nova Poshta 的分拣终端受到攻击，零售连锁店 ATB 和糖果集团 Roshen 拥有的仓库也受到攻击。

由于前线局势现已接近僵局，乌克兰的远程无人机袭击袭击了补给路线和能源设施，实际上切断了所吞并的克里米亚的联系，并导致俄罗斯各地的燃料短缺。

在仓库袭击之前，企业就已经受到干扰，但现在有些企业一夜之间失去了大部分库存。

据估计，俄罗斯 85-90% 的经济活跃人口都使用 Wildberry 及其竞争对手 Ozon。 在许多缺乏便利商店的偏远地区，俄罗斯人的大部分购物都依赖它们，从衣服到家用电器。

克里姆林宫否认在线平台用于军事供应，但防弹衣等各种军事相关商品以及民用和无人机生产应用的商品都在出售。

支持战争的博主表示，志愿者通常依靠这些平台购买装备，然后将其送往前线。

然而，没有独立证实袭击的目标仓库储存了这些物资。

与西方零售巨头亚马逊直接与其市场业务一起销售商品不同，Wildberry 和 Ozon 几乎完全充当市场，将独立卖家与俄罗斯各地的买家联系起来，存储和交付货物并处理付款。

这意味着攻击造成的许多损失是由企业承担的。

对于一位销售文具的女士来说，Wildberry 是唯一的分销渠道。 上周五，她将货物运送到莫斯科附近的 Elektrostal 仓库的第二天，她得知所有货物都已付之一炬。

她还剩下其他库存，但每年的这个时候对她的生意很重要，因为家长和学生会为新的秋季学期备货。

另一位销售青少年服装的 Wildberry 商人告诉 BBC，她损失了价值约 140 万卢布（13,300 英镑）的库存。 她将停止新供应几周。

“与 Wildberry 合作的前景非常暗淡，”她说。 “现在我有足够的安全缓冲来承受当前的损失，但如果有更多的罢工，我可能无法应对。”

两个女人都不期望得到补偿。 本月早些时候，距离袭击事件发生不到两周，他们的合同中添加了一项条款，其中将无人机袭击列入“不可抗力事件”清单，而 Wildberry 对此不承担责任。

公司老板塔蒂亚娜·金 (Tatiana Kim) 承诺“不会放弃”卖家，并向一些小企业支付了一些补偿。 她还提供了临时折扣并免除了运费，但这并没有缓解人们对进一步袭击的担忧。

即使在战时，支持中小企业也一直是弗拉基米尔·普京宣布的优先事项之一，并被视为面对西方制裁时的韧性支柱。

但俄罗斯政府一直无法保护该行业免受战争的影响，自今年年初以来，企业遭受了数次冲击。

1 月份增值税从 20% 上涨至 22%，额外资金专门用于国防，并且取消了一些企业的税收优惠。

根据商业智能平台 Kontur.Fokus 的数据，2026 年第一季度约有 209,000 家中小企业倒闭。 这比 2025 年前三个月增加了 9%。

据一些估计，大范围的互联网关闭和对流行通讯应用程序的打击导致了进一步的麻烦，莫斯科企业在 3 月份的一周内损失了数千万美元。

随后乌克兰对油库、炼油厂和供应路线的袭击引发了燃料危机。

“已经有很多钉子了，他们还在不断地钉钉子。 [Wildberry 仓库袭击事件] 是又一个致命一击吗？ 嗯，是的，当然，”巴塞罗那庞培法布拉大学的鲁本·埃尼克洛波夫教授告诉英国广播公司。

过去，俄罗斯的小企业表现出了韧性，但通货膨胀率很高，预算赤字不断扩大，石油和天然气收入比去年前六个月下降了23%。

俄罗斯拥有和平时期积累的巨额财政储备，但其在乌克兰的战争正在日益吞噬资源，导致民用经济增长乏力。

奥尔加·沙米娜的补充报道。
